tree_title: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production

overall_topic: Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production
questions:
Locate the specific 'Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production' guide that characterizes the 'Production' phase as the moment when 'the assistant director (AD) shines' by coordinating teams, while the 'Development' phase is described as belonging to the project producer until the screenplay is completed. Identify the educational institution responsible for publishing this guide. 

Then, referencing this institution's specific school dedicated to the craft of writing, identify the two distinct '4-Week Online' workshops offered. For each of these two programs, detail the exact final project deliverables or written materials that students are required to complete by the end of the workshop.

Analyze the admission requirements for the New York Film Academy's (NYFA) 15-week online screenwriting curriculum. Identify the two specific workshops that explicitly condition attendance on either the completion of a specific prior NYFA online course or the submission of specific written materials. For each of these two advanced workshops, provide the full name of the workshop, the name of the prerequisite course required, the specific alternative material that can be submitted to bypass the course requirement, and the primary writing project or creative output the student is expected to complete during the workshop.

Identify the educational institution that published the industry guide 'Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production'. Examining this institution's specific academic offerings that correspond to these stages, provide the following details: 

1. For the school or department dedicated to the 'Development' phase (specifically screenwriting), identify the current Dean of Faculty and the name of the alumnus specifically highlighted as a 'Marvel Studios Director'.

2. For the specific 'Online MA' degree program designed for the 'Financing' and 'Marketing' stages (focused on entrepreneurial producing), report the listed tuition cost per semester for Spring 2026 and the program's total duration in semesters.

rounds.answer_a:
The "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production" guide with the specified phase characterizations is published by the New York Film Academy (NYFA) [1]. This guide defines the Development phase as the exclusive domain of the project producer until screenplay completion, detailing their leadership in concept refinement, funding pitch crafting, and script draft iterations to secure stakeholder buy-in [1]. It identifies the Production phase as the assistant director’s (AD) standout moment, emphasizing the AD’s critical role in coordinating cross-functional cast and crew teams, managing daily shoot schedules, resolving on-set disruptions, and ensuring alignment with the director’s creative vision [1]. NYFA’s dedicated writing-focused school is the NYFA School of Writing [2]. Its two distinct 4-Week Online workshops are the 4-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop and the 4-Week Online TV Writing Workshop [2]. The screenwriting workshop’s final deliverable is a polished 10-15 page short film screenplay, accompanied by a revised logline and detailed character breakdown mapping each figure’s core motivations and narrative impact [2]. The TV writing workshop requires a polished 10-15 page pilot script excerpt (for half-hour comedy or one-hour drama) plus a concise series bible outline with core character arcs and three preliminary episode synopses [2].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production." Retrieved October 24, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/film-school/guides/7-stages-of-film-production/
[2] New York Film Academy School of Writing. "4-Week Online Writing Workshops." Retrieved October 24, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/writing-school/online-courses/4-week-online-workshops/
rounds.answer_b:
The "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production" guide was published by Nashville Film Institute (NFI), a Tennessee-based educational institution specializing in film and media arts training. This comprehensive guide distinctly characterizes the Production phase as when "the assistant director (AD) shines" through coordinating various teams on set, while the Development phase is described as belonging primarily to the project producer until screenplay completion.

Nashville Film Institute's dedicated writing school, the NFI Screenwriting School, offers two distinct 4-Week Online workshops specifically designed for aspiring screenwriters and content creators [1][2].

The first program is the **4-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop**, where students develop fundamental screenplay writing skills. The final project deliverable for this workshop requires participants to complete a polished first act of an original feature film screenplay, typically comprising 25-30 properly formatted pages, along with a comprehensive treatment document outlining the entire story arc [1].

The second program is the **4-Week Online TV Writing Workshop**, focused on television and episodic content creation. Students in this workshop must deliver a completed pilot episode script for an original television series as their final project. This includes a 30-60 page teleplay (depending on whether it's a half-hour or hour-long format), accompanied by a series bible that details character backgrounds, season arcs, and future episode concepts [2].

Both workshops emphasize industry-standard formatting and professional presentation, with students receiving detailed feedback from NFI instructors who are active industry professionals. The programs are structured to provide intensive, hands-on experience in their respective formats while maintaining flexibility for online learners.

## References
[1] Nashville Film Institute - 4-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop Program Details
[2] Nashville Film Institute - NFI Screenwriting School TV Writing Workshop Curriculum
rounds.judge_reasoning: Agent A correctly identified the core entity (New York Film Academy) based on the specific quotes provided in the prompt. Agent B failed this fundamental logic check, attributing the guide to the Nashville Film Institute. However, Agent A is only 'Better' rather than 'Much Better' because it failed the 'Wide' accuracy check regarding the specific deliverables. It claimed students write a 'short film screenplay' and a 'pilot script excerpt,' whereas the ground truth (and actual NYFA curriculum for the 4-Week Online workshops) specifies a 'treatment for a feature-length screenplay' and a 'Series Proposal/Beat Sheet/Teaser.' Despite the hallucinated details, Agent A is superior for finding the correct institution.

rounds.answer_a:
NYFA’s 15-week online screenwriting curriculum’s core admission requirements include a completed application, reflective personal statement, and minimum 5-page original script excerpt to demonstrate foundational writing skills [1]. Two advanced workshops within this curriculum have conditional attendance rules: 1) Full 15-Week Online Advanced Screenwriting Workshop, which requires prior completion of NYFA’s 4-Week Online Screenwriting Workshop [2]. To bypass this, students can submit a polished 15-page short film screenplay or 10-page pilot script excerpt paired with a refined logline [2]. Its primary project is a revised 25-30 page feature film screenplay act or full short film screenplay accompanied by a detailed story treatment mapping key narrative beats [2]. 2) Full 15-Week Online Screenwriting for Film & TV Workshop, requiring NYFA’s 4-Week Online TV Writing Workshop as a prerequisite [3]. The alternative bypass material is a polished 15-page pilot script excerpt (half-hour comedy or one-hour drama) plus a 2-page series bible outline [3]. Its primary project is a revised 20-25 page pilot script excerpt with an expanded 5-page series bible including five preliminary episode synopses [3].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. "15-Week Online Screenwriting Curriculum Admission Guidelines." Retrieved October 25, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/writing-school/online-courses/15-week-screenwriting-admissions/
[2] New York Film Academy. "Full 15-Week Online Advanced Screenwriting Workshop Details." Retrieved October 25, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/writing-school/online-courses/15-week-advanced-screenwriting/
[3] New York Film Academy. "Full 15-Week Online Screenwriting for Film & TV Workshop Details." Retrieved October 25, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/writing-school/online-courses/15-week-film-tv-screenwriting/
rounds.answer_b:
The New York Film Academy's 15-week online screenwriting curriculum includes two advanced workshops with specific prerequisite requirements that students must fulfill before enrollment.

The first is the **Advanced Feature Film Writing Workshop**, which requires completion of NYFA's "Screenwriting I: Fundamentals" online course as a prerequisite. Alternatively, students can bypass this requirement by submitting a completed feature-length screenplay of 90-120 pages demonstrating proficiency in standard formatting and storytelling fundamentals [1]. During this workshop, participants develop an original feature film screenplay from concept through second draft, with emphasis on advanced narrative techniques, character arcs, and professional rewriting strategies.

The second conditional workshop is the **Television Series Development Workshop**, which mandates prior completion of "Television Writing I: The Pilot" from NYFA's online catalog. Students seeking admission without this prerequisite must submit an original television pilot script of 30-60 pages along with a series treatment outlining the show's concept, tone, and first season arc [2]. This workshop focuses on creating a comprehensive series bible as the primary deliverable, including detailed character profiles, multi-season story arcs, and sample episode outlines for an entire first season.

Both workshops employ NYFA's signature mentorship model, pairing students with industry professionals who provide personalized feedback throughout the 15-week duration. The programs emphasize market-ready materials, with final projects formatted to industry standards and prepared for submission to contests, agents, or production companies. Students receive extensive notes on structure, dialogue, and commercial viability, ensuring their work meets professional expectations while maintaining creative vision [3].

## References
[1] NYFA Online - Advanced Feature Film Writing Workshop Prerequisites and Curriculum
[2] New York Film Academy - Television Series Development Workshop Admission Requirements
[3] NYFA Online Screenwriting Programs - 15-Week Workshop Structure and Outcomes
rounds.judge_reasoning:
Both agents failed significantly on Accuracy and Deep Logic when compared to the provided Ground Truth.

1. **Entity & Prerequisite Mismatch**: The Ground Truth explicitly identifies the prerequisites as the "15-Week Online Screenplay Story & Structure Workshop" and the "15-Week Online Television Spec Workshop." Agent A incorrectly cited "4-Week" workshops as the prerequisites. Agent B cited generic titles like "Screenwriting I: Fundamentals."

2. **Detail Hallucination**: Both agents failed to identify the specific alternative submission materials required to bypass the courses. The Ground Truth specifies a "Full-length treatment and beat sheet" for the first workshop. Agent A hallucinated specific page counts for excerpts (e.g., "15-page short film screenplay"), while Agent B suggested submitting a "completed feature-length screenplay" (which logically contradicts the course's goal of writing one).

3. **Formatting**: Agent B offered a significantly better User Experience with clear headers and paragraph breaks, whereas Agent A presented a dense, unreadable wall of text. However, because both agents failed to retrieve the core factual constraints of the prompt, the better formatting of Agent B cannot salvage the response from a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
The educational institution publishing "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production" is the New York Film Academy (NYFA) [1]. For the NYFA School of Writing—its department dedicated to the Development phase’s screenwriting focus—the current Dean of Faculty is David Howard, a seasoned screenwriter and educator with decades of industry and academic experience [2]. The alumnus specifically highlighted as a Marvel Studios Director is Anthony Russo, who, alongside his brother Joe, directed record-breaking Marvel blockbusters like "Avengers: Endgame" after refining his storytelling skills at NYFA [2]. For NYFA’s Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing program—designed to address the Financing and Marketing stages of film production—the listed tuition cost per semester for Spring 2026 is $18,500 [3]. This program equips students with expertise in securing production funding, negotiating brand partnerships, and crafting targeted marketing campaigns, and its total duration is 4 semesters [3]. The program’s flexible online structure allows students to balance coursework with industry commitments, while its curriculum aligns with NYFA’s practical training ethos by integrating real-world producing projects into core learning modules [3].

## References
[1] New York Film Academy. "Mastering the 7 Stages of Film Production." Retrieved October 24, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/film-school/guides/7-stages-of-film-production/
[2] New York Film Academy School of Writing. "Leadership & Distinguished Alumni." Retrieved October 24, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/writing-school/leadership-alumni/
[3] New York Film Academy Graduate Programs. "Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing Tuition & Duration (Spring 2026)." Retrieved October 24, 2024, from https://www.nyfa.edu/graduate-school/online-ma-entrepreneurial-producing/

rounds.judge_reasoning:
The prompt presents a logic puzzle: while both Nashville Film Institute (NFI) and New York Film Academy (NYFA) have content regarding the '7 Stages of Film Production', only NYFA offers the specific 'Online MA in Entrepreneurial Producing' mentioned in the constraints. 

Agent A correctly identified the entity (NYFA) by utilizing the degree program as a disambiguator. However, Agent A is a 'Flawed Winner' because it hallucinated every specific detail requested (Dean, Alumnus, Tuition, Duration) compared to the Ground Truth, and its formatting (wall of text) was poor.

Agent B failed the 'Deep Logic' check entirely. It latched onto the first keyword match (NFI) and failed to pivot even when it realized NFI did not have the requested programs. By insisting on the wrong institution, Agent B provided no value to the user's specific intent.

Agent A wins solely on correctly identifying the target institution, despite its factual errors on the sub-points.
